title: SaaS Platform License Agreement between CloudVault Technologies and Meridian Financial Group

drafting_sequence.clause_text: 'Service' means the CloudVault enterprise data management platform, including all associated APIs, dashboards, storage infrastructure, and documentation. 'Subscription Term' means the period during which Client has authorized access to the Service.

Provider grants Client a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to access and use the Service during the Subscription Term solely for Client's internal business operations. Client shall not sublicense, resell, or distribute access to the Service to any third party.

Provider guarantees 99.95% monthly uptime availability for the Service, measured as total minutes in the calendar month minus unplanned downtime minutes, divided by total minutes. Scheduled maintenance windows of up to four (4) hours per month shall be excluded from uptime calculations.

If monthly uptime falls below the guaranteed 99.95% threshold, Client shall receive a service credit equal to 15% of the monthly subscription fee for each full 0.1% below the threshold, up to a maximum credit of 100% of the monthly fee. Credits shall be applied to the next billing cycle automatically.

All data uploaded, stored, processed, or generated by Client through the Service ('Client Data') shall remain the exclusive property of Client. Provider acquires no rights, title, or interest in Client Data and shall not access, use, analyze, or monetize Client Data for any purpose other than providing the Service.

Provider shall implement and maintain industry-standard security measures including AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and annual third-party penetration testing. Provider shall notify Client of any data breach within twenty-four (24) hours of discovery.

This Agreement shall have an initial Subscription Term of twenty-four (24) months commencing on the Effective Date. The Agreement shall automatically renew for successive twelve (12) month periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the then-current term.

Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) commits a material breach and fails to cure within thirty (30) days after written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) ceases its business operations.

Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) arising from: (a) Provider's breach of this Agreement; (b) any infringement of third-party intellectual property rights by the Service; or (c) Provider's negligence or willful misconduct. There shall be no cap on Provider's indemnification obligations.

In no event shall either party's total aggregate liability under this Agreement exceed the total fees paid by Client during the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim. Neither party shall be liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 09 explicitly states 'no cap on Provider's indemnification obligations'. Clause 10 caps all aggregate liability at 12 months of fees. These are directly incompatible — indemnification cannot be simultaneously uncapped and capped.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Client shall have the right to audit Provider's data handling practices, security controls, and compliance with this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice. Audits shall be conducted during normal business hours and at Client's expense. Provider shall cooperate fully with any audit.

Client grants Provider a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to collect, aggregate, analyze, and create derivative works from Client Data for the purposes of improving the Service, developing new features, training machine learning models, generating anonymized industry benchmarks, and conducting internal research.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 05 prohibits Provider from accessing, using, analyzing, or monetizing Client Data for any purpose other than providing the Service. Clause 12 grants Provider a license to analyze Client Data for ML training, benchmarks, and research — purposes explicitly forbidden by clause 05.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause by providing thirty (30) calendar days written notice. Upon such termination, Client shall pay all fees accrued through the effective date of termination but shall not be liable for any early termination penalties or remaining subscription fees.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 07 requires 90 days notice for non-renewal, implying the contract cannot be exited with shorter notice. Clause 13 allows either party to terminate at any time with only 30 days notice, effectively nullifying the 90-day non-renewal window.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under this Agreement if such failure or delay results from circumstances beyond the reasonable control of that party, including but not limited to acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, pandemics, government orders, or failure of third-party infrastructure providers.

In the event of a security breach affecting Client Data, Provider shall notify Client within seventy-two (72) hours of confirming the breach. The notification shall include the nature and scope of the breach, the data affected, and the remediation steps being taken.
drafting_sequence.contradiction_description: Clause 06 requires breach notification within 24 hours of discovery. Clause 15 allows 72 hours after confirmation. These are incompatible timelines — 24 hours from discovery is significantly stricter than 72 hours from confirmation.

drafting_sequence.clause_text: Provider shall maintain cyber liability insurance with coverage limits of not less than $5,000,000 per occurrence and $10,000,000 in the aggregate, as well as professional liability insurance of $5,000,000 per claim.

Upon termination of this Agreement, Provider shall make all Client Data available for export in industry-standard formats (CSV, JSON, or XML) within fourteen (14) business days. Provider shall delete all copies of Client Data within thirty (30) days of the termination effective date.

Provider shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards including but not limited to GDPR, CCPA, SOX, and HIPAA to the extent they are applicable to the processing of Client Data. Provider shall provide compliance certifications upon request.

During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months following termination, neither party shall directly or indirectly solicit, recruit, or hire any employee of the other party who was involved in the performance of this Agreement.

This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York. Any dispute arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City under the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association.
